fact: November 8 is exactly 42 days before winter solstice in the Northern Hemisphere - that's the same number as the answer to everything in The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy!
Squirrels go absolutely bonkers around this time, burying up to 25 nuts per hour as they prep for winter - they're like tiny, furry doomsday preppers!
In most places north of the equator, sunrise happens after 7 AM for the first time since spring, making mornings feel extra sleepy and dark.
Tree sap starts moving super slowly right now, which is why maple trees save up all their sugar for spring syrup - they're basically tree batteries charging up!
November 8 falls smack in the middle of 'Aurora Season' in northern countries, when the nights get long enough and dark enough for the best Northern Lights shows.
Spiders start invading houses like crazy around this date because outdoor temperatures drop below their comfort zone - they're basically eight-legged refugees!
The moon looks extra huge and orange when it rises around this time of year because of the angle it takes through Earth's thick, dusty autumn atmosphere.
Deer grow their thickest winter coats right around now, adding up to 5,000 extra hairs per square inch - that's like wearing 20 sweaters at once!
In the Southern Hemisphere, November 8 marks peak nesting season for penguins, who are busy building rock mansions and doing silly dances to impress their mates.
Frost starts forming on grass even when air temperature is above freezing around this date, because the ground radiates heat faster than air on clear nights.
November 8 sits right in the 'Taurid meteor shower' peak, when Earth plows through a cloud of space debris that creates slow, bright fireballs across the sky.
Bears in cold regions enter their deepest pre-hibernation eating frenzy around now, consuming up to 40,000 calories per day - that's like eating 80 cheeseburgers!
